decision_claim: Autonomous continuation after GAP-113 must choose the next work item from gate status, source/evidence freshness, and LTP promotion policy. It must not start a heavy LTP package when the LTP policy returns defer_all.

selection_order.why: Broken build/test/contract/hotspot gates invalidate planning or promotion work.

selection_order.condition: source_state == fresh && evidence_state == stale && evidence_blocker == host_capability_degraded_bounded_defer
selection_order.why: Fresh target-run evidence still proves a bounded host capability defer; wait for a Codex build or host posture that can prove native_attach recovery instead of repeating evidence refresh.
Claims and source assumptions must be fresh before selecting implementation work.
Exactly one LTP package has trigger evidence and a scope fence.
Owner-directed heavy-stack work is allowed only with explicit labeling and scope.
No heavy LTP package is currently trigger-selected; continue with evidence freshness and gate posture.

invariants: Gate repair outranks new implementation work.
Stale source, claim, or evidence posture outranks heavy-stack promotion.
The next-work selector must consume the LTP promotion evaluator instead of reinterpreting LTP package fields directly.
The selector must never convert defer_all into LTP implementation work.
The selector must preserve build -> test -> contract/invariant -> hotspot as the acceptance floor.
rollback_ref: git revert the autonomous next-work selection policy, selector, tests, gate wiring, planning docs, backlog, seeds, claim-catalog entry, and evidence
